headline: Selangor Groundwater Extraction Exceeds Recharge by 180%
context: JMG Selangor's 2025 hydrogeological study found groundwater extraction in the Klang Valley exceeds natural recharge rates by 180%, causing land subsidence in 4 districts.

cards.big: Klang Valley pumps out 180% more groundwater than rainfall can replace.
cards.sub: 4 districts show measurable land subsidence — up to 3cm per year in Petaling.

cards.big: 2,340 registered groundwater wells operate in the Klang Valley.
cards.sub: An estimated 800 unregistered wells also operate, mostly serving factories and car washes.

cards.big: Land subsidence has cracked foundations in 140 buildings in Petaling.
cards.sub: Insurance does not cover subsidence. Repair costs average RM85,000 per affected property.

cards.big: Jakarta sinks 25cm per year due to groundwater extraction.
cards.sub: Klang Valley's current trajectory follows Jakarta's pattern — 30 years behind but on the same curve.

cards.big: We are pumping the ground from under our own buildings. The subsidence is not mysterious — it is predictable.
Groundwater extraction licensing must be enforced, metered, and priced to reflect scarcity. The alternative — watched from Jakarta — is a sinking city.
